reviews.review_msg: Lectures are quite dry and he took out majority of the math. Wastes time in lecture answering pointless questions with little relevance to econ. Explanations were quite lacking or convoluted. Added in lectures that were not originally in the syllabus. HW marking standards were weird. Exams were ok but he could forget to mark yours. Nice guy tho.
Funny guy, engages with students. Explained concepts well, if a little bit slowly. Tests and final were fair. All around great prof.

Eliav can be unorganized sometimes, but he is very kind. TA's for this class can be unhelpful sometimes, but I noticed the course was based around his lectures so quizzes we're easy, practical and fair. Midterm and final wasn't horrible just make sure you review.

Macroeconomics was not an easy course, however, it was not difficult to achieve a high grade in this class. he gave us problem sets that are easy to get 100% on if you try, because they are not marked for correct answers but for completion. We also had quizzes that were a little bit tough but doable and there were no surprises on his exams.

Currently taking Econ 105 with him and he is a great professor. He has been really helpful and really wants his students to do well. Lectures are a bit boring, but only because most 3 hour lectures are boring in general, so you can't blame him. Glad I took his class

He is good but he is very disorganized and his exams do not cover everything from lectures so some questions are confusing. However, he gives good participation marks and show your work marks which I like and he seems nice. However, he can be intimidating to approach.

He seems to have changed the material he teaches so it has become so much easier. He has a good sense of humour and makes lectures go fast and even let people go sooner when he finishes the lecture one hour early. The course has similar things to Econ 103 so if you liked it, take this course.

Doesn't use Canvas for grades, so you're completely lost for that. Gets distracted in lectures, quizzes are okay, but exams are insane. One of his TA's is impatient and rude. His final is accumulative, so you have to remember formulas from his first lecture. Dude is completely disorganized, do not take his class.

Final exam is totally not relevant to the material. Is all about your imagination, his lecture won't help, his practice exam won't help, his assignments won't help. I you want four midterms and an F take Econ392 with Eliav Danzige. The worse professor ever.

This was one of the most disorganized classes I've ever taken. Eliav doesn't even use canvas, so I'll have no idea where I stand in this CURVED class until I get my final mark. Additionally, he doesn't even have a suggested textbook so I couldn't pre-read before class without searching through a random Macroeconomics textbook to find the topics.

He is a newer professor and is still ironing out techniques however, it seems he is really trying to teach the material and to be interesting. Sometimes he would go on tangents, and spend too much lecture time on "dumb questions" but his tests were fair. Would take another class with him for sure.
dont take his class if you havent done economics before, he has no clear syllabus,confusing explanations,he is funny but his classes are boring, disorganized etc
absolutely useless, waste of tuition. Might as well never go to any class, stay at home an just look at his powerpoint and other youtube videos because his explanations are pretty weak and lacking

His lectures are well-related to the readings and decent. He tends to allow too many questions, slowing down the lectures and making the syllabus schedule wonky. His exams are fair. 2/3 of the exam are questions that are identical to the assignments he gives. The last 1/3rd require a deeper understanding of the stuff and creative application.

No clear syllabus so you're guessing at what to read prior to class. He feels somewhat scattered and unprepared in his lectures which leaves students a bit confused. He seems to try to trick students on exams with questions which are more difficult than assignments. This killed my grade on the final. He seems to forget this is a 1st year class.

Took Econ 105 with him. The way he teaches is very good but the stuff he puts on the midterm final is pretty much out of nowhere. I read all the chapters from the book and I must say it was completely useful. I barely ended up passing the class. If you want a good grade, do not take it with him.

Took this class for the second time, ended up getting a way better grade, but I had to mark much harder. Midterm average for the class was about 50%, but since the class is curved that didn't matter much. This was Eliav's first time teaching, and I really like him. Funny and approachable. Class is tough though. Don't skip lectures, you WILL fail.

People complain that we didn't learn some of the questions.  But that's how you separate a "C" student from an "A" student - you ask them to think logically and apply the material from lecture.  Super clear on the concepts in lecture, helps a lot during OH, and it's relatively easy if you study enough - finished wan A+.  Danziger's a boss.

Lectures very similar to textbook material; very clear in articulating concepts. Nice guy, willing to help people, can tell he has interest in economics. Tests were fair for the most part besides one final question that we may not have learned.

Definitely not a good prof, makes tons of mistakes during lecture and stumbles when trying to correct himself because he doesn't even know the right answer either. Went to every lecture and it did not help with the final, pulls questions out of the air with no background in the class.

He's a new prof, and it definitely shows. His lectures aren't boring, but they aren't very informative, just textbook stuff. His exams are hard, and he sometimes puts stuff on them that were not covered in lecture or the textbook.
Eliav is a new teacher, he never writes anything on the notes
This was my favorite class this semester.   Eliav made the lectures go by quickly.  He's a great teacher and tells funny jokes.  My TA was not great though.
Mandatory class, my TA was not the best. Eliav was very helpful when I needed him.
